before.description: Unified atomic git workflow with GitHub integration — validates, commits conventionally, checks issues, creates PR, monitors ALL Actions with pre-existing detection, uses swarm/web research, auto-merges with strategy selection, and post-merge validates. Use this skill when the user asks to commit code, create a PR, push changes, merge, or manage the full git lifecycle — even if they just say 'push it' or 'ship it'.

after.description: Orchestrates the full git-to-merge lifecycle: validate → commit → check issues → create PR → monitor ALL GitHub Actions (including pre-existing failures) → fix via swarm/web research → merge with strategy selection → post-merge validate. Use this skill when the user asks to ship changes end-to-end, manage a PR through CI, or handle the complete commit-to-merge workflow — even if they just say 'push it' or 'ship it'. Not for simple one-off git operations (revert, squash, cherry-pick) or isolated tasks (just review, just test, just lint).

key_changes: Added lifecycle flow diagram (validate → commit → ... → validate)
Changed 'commit code, create a PR, push changes, merge' to 'ship changes end-to-end, manage a PR through CI'
Added negative triggers: 'Not for simple one-off git operations (revert, squash, cherry-pick) or isolated tasks'
Added 'even if they don't explicitly mention all phases' implicit via 'end-to-end'

trigger_tests.query: review this PR for code quality
set up a new CI/CD pipeline
